metadata.title: Build a Simulation with the Three-File System with Claude Code

props.topic: HUMANITARIANS AI · Build a Simulation with the Three-File System with Claude Code

beats.narration: Build me a sorting simulator produces Material Design blue and drag-and-drop. Three files written first produce your class colors and single-click stepping.

beats.narration_text: Here is why Build a Simulation with the Three-File System with Claude Code belongs in your working knowledge. The systems, policies, and tools shaped by it affect decisions at your level — whether or not you understand the mechanism. Knowing the underlying logic gives you the frame; missing it means reasoning from output with no access to the inputs.

beats.narration: The three-file system is what prevents a generic demo from landing in your classroom. You decide the colors, the interaction model, and the pedagogy before Claude sees the project. The three files are the decisions.
Ask Claude to generate a single-file sorting simulator from three constraint documents — CLAUDE.md, DESIGN.md, and PROJECT.md.

props.command:
claude "Using three files in context:
  CLAUDE.md: vanilla JS, single-file, no framework
  DESIGN.md: earth tones #8B7355 #6B8E6B,
    single-click stepping, 44px touch targets
  PROJECT.md: bubble/merge/quick sort,
    comparison + swap counts, ninth grade
Generate index.html — single deployable file.
No color not in DESIGN.md.
No drag-and-drop."
props.topic: HUMANITARIANS AI · CLAUDE CODE FOR TEACHERS

beats.narration: The generated index.html uses the earth-tone palette from DESIGN.md, vanilla JS only from CLAUDE.md, and the step-button with 44-pixel minimum height for touch targets.

props.code: Comparisons: 0 Swaps: 0
props.title: index.html (excerpt)

beats.narration: The generated simulator uses the earth-tone palette exactly. No framework imports. Single-click step button with 44-pixel minimum height. Comparison count and swap count displayed. The class colors are in the CSS because they were in DESIGN.md — not because Claude chose them.
Add a contrast requirement to DESIGN.md and regenerate to see which colors change.

props.command:
claude "Modify DESIGN.md:
  add contrast requirement:
  all text must have 4.5:1 contrast ratio.
Re-run generation — which colors change?"
props.topic: HUMANITARIANS AI · CLAUDE CODE FOR TEACHERS

beats.narration: The regenerated simulator adjusts the text color on the accent background from #6B8E6B to #3D5C3D to meet 4.5:1 contrast. Claude updated the specific color that failed the contrast check without changing the primary palette or the interaction model. The DESIGN.md constraint propagated automatically.

The three-file system makes the simulation yours: CLAUDE.md constrains the technical implementation, DESIGN.md constrains the aesthetics, PROJECT.md constrains the pedagogy. Claude generates inside all three constraints without needing to be reminded of any of them.
Next: run the three-check deployment verification protocol.
